title: Slot Slasher on Nail Help
game_context: 4-on-4 half-court shell. The point drives middle from the slot. Your defender (x_slot) is the weak-side slot help — he steps to the nail (top of key, middle of the lane line) to dig at the drive. The strong-side elbow opens.
possession_setup: You are the weak-side slot. The point drives middle. Your defender (x_slot) steps to the nail — feet on the lane line at the foul-line extended, hand digging at the ball.
decision_moment: x_slot has stepped to the nail. His chest is to the ball, hand reaching at the dribble, feet inside the lane line. The strong-side elbow is empty.
visible_cue: x_slot has both feet inside the lane line, head and chest to the ball, the strong-side elbow has no defender between you and the rim.
prompt: Your defender stepped to the nail. The strong-side elbow is empty.

choices.label: Slash to the strong-side elbow.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. He stepped to the nail and gave up the elbow. Slash there — the point reads it and finds you in stride.

choices.label: Lift to the top of the key for the shot.
choices.feedback_text: Okay. The lift is open if he doesn't recover, but he is one step closer to the top than to the elbow. The slash is faster.
choices.partial_feedback_text: Lift to the top is a fine option. The cleaner read was the strong-side elbow.

choices.label: Stand at your slot and call for the kickout.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Standing still lets x_slot recover. The kickout is contested.

choices.label: Cut behind the drive to the strong-side dunker.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Cutting behind the drive runs into x_wing's help and stays in the same crowd as the ball.

best_read: Plant the inside foot and slash diagonally to the strong-side elbow. The point reads the help and pockets a one-handed pass — the catch is in shooting pocket and the closeout from x_slot has to cover 12 ft of court.
acceptable_reads: Lift to the top of the key — open if x_slot is slow to recover, but the slash to the elbow is faster.
bad_reads: Stand still — x_slot recovers and the catch is contested.
Cut behind the drive — runs into x_wing and the strong-side help.
common_miss_reason: Slot players default to spotting up at the top. The cue is the help going forward — once the defender steps to the nail, the slash to the elbow is faster than the lift up.
why_best_read_works: The nail step takes x_slot's recovery foot away from your slot. The elbow is closer to your slot than to x_slot's nail position; you arrive first. x_wing cannot leave the ball-side wing without giving up the corner shot.
decoder_teaching_point: When help steps to the nail, the strong-side elbow is the closest empty area. Slash, don't lift.

feedback.correct: Good read. He stepped to the nail; you slashed to the elbow and got the layup.
feedback.partial: Lifting to the top is a fine option. Next time, look at where his feet went — if they're at the nail, the elbow is closer.
feedback.wrong: Watch the nail. If his feet are at the nail, the strong-side elbow is yours.

self_review_checklist: Did I see his feet step to the nail?
Did I cut to the elbow, not the top?
Did I catch in shooting pocket?
Did I show my hands above the elbow?

coach_validation.notes: Slash to elbow on nail help is universally taught. Difficulty 2 because the player must distinguish nail vs full help (a high-vs-low cue). Cluster fits beginner cap.

source_research_basis: Universally taught youth read; slash to elbow against nail help is system-independent.
explanation_md: **Empty-Space Cut — nail-help variant.** When your defender steps to the nail, his recovery foot is on the wrong side of you. Plant your inside foot and slash to the strong-side elbow. The catch is in shooting pocket. Watch his feet, not the ball.

wrongDemos.caption: You lifted to the top. x_slot recovered and the shot was contested.
You stayed put. x_slot recovered and the kickout was tipped.
You cut behind the drive. x_wing met you in the strong-side dunker.
